name: CNC fixture with all clamp-bolt approaches blocked
intent: Represent a synthetic machining fixture whose straight and angled driver approaches are each obstructed by installed machine hardware.

parts.name: Machined fixture plate
parts.purpose: Locate the workpiece and carry the clamp bolt

parts.name: Fixture clamp bolt
parts.purpose: Clamp the workpiece against the fixture datum

fastener.basis: Declared by the authored BOM for this synthetic failure fixture.

parts.name: Fixed splash guard
parts.purpose: Protect the operator from coolant and chips

parts.name: Vise support column
parts.purpose: Support the installed vise beside the fixture plate

tool_access_cones.name: Clamp bolt straight driver approach
tool_access_cones.rationale: The nominal straight approach runs into the fixed splash guard.

tool_access_cones.name: Clamp bolt angled driver approach
tool_access_cones.rationale: The alternative diagonal approach runs into the installed vise support column.

assumptions: This is an authored synthetic failure fixture; it does not represent downloaded or external source CAD.
The fixture plate is described as CNC-machined, but this fixture checks tool access only and makes no machining-feasibility claim.
Every envelope is an intentionally conservative axis-aligned proxy in millimetres.
The two tool approaches are authored alternatives; flexible tools, disassembly, and machine motion are outside this static check.
